source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Lost Time Injury

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Galveston Area Block A 244

actions_taken: Platform personnel stabilized the injured person
Injured person transported to University of Texas Medical Branch Galveston via helicopter after approximately 2 hours
Injured person placed in stokes litter and lifted to helideck using crane
event.summary: On February 7, 2024, at approximately 09:30 am, a Danos contractor sustained a lost time injury (broken leg and ankle) when an inadequately built scaffolding ladder shifted while he was climbing it on the third rung. The ladder had been placed out of service on January 1, 2024, when the Self-Retracting Lanyard and scaffold tag were removed, but no visual identifiers marked it as OOS. The ladder had only two attachment points and was improperly constructed. The injured person jumped to the deck when the ladder shifted, sustaining injuries to both lower legs. Multiple systemic failures contributed including inadequate communication between operators and contractors, lack of bridging documents, no operator involvement in JSA processes, improper equipment inspection and maintenance, and inadequate fall protection equipment.
recommendations: All operators review or revisit Safety Alert No. 469 (BSEE Identifies Medical Evacuation and Emergency Hazards During Risk-Based Inspections)
key_phrases: inadequate communication between operator and platform personnel/contractors

threats.name: Lack of visual identification of out-of-service equipment
Inadequate communication between operators and contractors
Improper selection and inspection of fall protection equipment
Lack of operator involvement in contractor JSA process

consequences.name: Personnel injury - broken leg and ankle

controls.name: Scaffolding inspection and tagging system
controls.lod_basis: First line administrative control to verify equipment status before use

supporting_text: In a typical scaffolding building process tags are usually accompanied with each item constructed, no tags associated with this scaffolding could be presented to investigators showing this ladder was inspected and maintained
Once the tag and SRL was removed there were no other visual identifiers letting platform personnel know this scaffolding ladder was placed OOS
Inadequate out of service tags for equipment there was no other visual identifiers letting platform personnel know this scaffolding ladder was placed OOS

controls.name: Scaffolding structural design and construction standards
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design standard to prevent structural failure

supporting_text: Flawed equipment construction the scaffold ladder anchor points were unconventional & not best practices. The scaffolding ladder had only two attached anchor points which contributed to its instability
The legs of the ladder were not on the same plane as the platform deck, allowing some means to stabilize the ladder from unequal deflection which contributed to its instability
A scaffolding ladder with a Self-Retracting Lanyard (SRL) was erected by Danos on the southwest corner of the EMPCO MCC Building
It was built and attached to the building with only one attachment point at the top and bottom

controls.name: Preventative maintenance program for scaffolding
controls.barrier_role: Maintain scaffolding integrity through regular inspection
controls.lod_basis: Routine maintenance to prevent equipment degradation

supporting_text: Inadequate preventative maintenance has been identified in the maintenance and inspection of built scaffolding on this facility
Not having these maintenance records/tags indicate the ladder was not being properly maintained or inspected

controls.name: Fall protection equipment inspection and certification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure fall protection equipment is suitable and functional

supporting_text: there was a harness being used with a tag that stated it was only to be used within a certain weight capacity range
the fall arrest equipment that was being used with the harness was rated for a higher weight capacity than the harness and was not attached correctly
there was no inspection records available for the harness and associated fall arrest equipment being used
Prior to job improper selection of safety equipment

controls.name: Self-Retracting Lanyard (SRL) at work at height location

supporting_text: After confirmation from EMPCO personnel the ladder was not in use a Danos scaffold builder removed the SRL and the scaffold tag from the scaffolding ladder, placing it out of service (OOS) on January 1, 2024
the SRL was removed from the top of the scaffold ladder/work area to be used in another area of the platform

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for work at heights
controls.barrier_role: Identify and control hazards before work begins

supporting_text: On February 7, 2024, at 06:30 am two Danos Job Safety Analysis (JSA's)Crane & Demo with power tools, an Exxon Work Permit, and Job Loss Analysis(JLA) were created. Each of the permits created did not mention working from heights
no discussion of working at heights was discussed during any meetings or work permit approvals
Please note that the work being performed at the time of the incident did not require the use of a ladder, or access to scaffolding, & working at heights
Each JSA was signed only by their respective personnel i.e., Danos personnel on Danos with Danos personnel not on Williams or ExxonMobil other contractor JSA's

controls.name: Operator (PIC) involvement in contractor work planning and JSA
controls.barrier_role: Ensure platform operator oversight of contractor activities

supporting_text: The Platform Right of Way is operated by Williams Oil Gathering (Person in Charge (PIC) is a Williams representative) with Exxon personnel onboard the platform. Interestingly, the PIC for the platform was not involved with either Exxon's or Danos's JSA process
Inadequate equipment inspection operators were not involved in the inspection process of the scaffolding ladder
Inadequate approval of work permits from platform lease operators not having any input into other JSA's created by other contractors

controls.name: Bridging document between operators and contractors
controls.barrier_role: Define roles, responsibilities, and communication protocols
controls.lod_basis: Management system to coordinate multi-party operations

supporting_text: Additionally, there is no bridging document between Exxon and Williams companies
No bridging documents between operators and contractors

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting between all parties
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate work activities and share hazard information

supporting_text: No pre-job safety meeting between all parties were four separate work permits created. One for Williams Pipeline (Work Permit), two for Danos (JSA), and one for EMPCO (JLA). Each contractor did not sign the other contractors JSA or were part of their respective toolbox/safety meeting on the platform

controls.name: Equipment pre-use inspection
controls.lod_basis: Worker verification at point of use

supporting_text: Prior to job equipment was not inspected
Inadequate knowledge of equipment status
Not aware of hazards

controls.name: Emergency medical evacuation procedures and resources
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through rapid medical response

supporting_text: After approximately two hours the Search and Rescue (SAR) helicopter arrived
One individual included in his statement that 'The helicopter arrived two hours after being called.'
All SAR assets come from Louisiana and the incident occurred off the coast of Texas
Regarding the evacuation of the IP from the platform, it took over two hours for the IP to be evacuated
It should be noted that the IP was not stabilized on a backboard, neck brace or splint prior to his evacuation from the platform
Depending on the platform subscribers tier level with SAR companies, the allowed SAR asset varies

controls.name: First aid and casualty stabilization
controls.barrier_role: Prevent further injury and stabilize casualty
controls.lod_basis: Immediate response to limit injury escalation

supporting_text: The platform personnel started to arrive where the injury took place, helping the IP across the deck to an area where he could be sat upright leaning against a deck box
when platform personnel realized the IP had a broken leg and ankle he was stabilized then search and rescue (SAR) operators were contacted to assist
It should be noted that the IP was not stabilized on a backboard, neck brace or splint prior to his evacuation from the platform

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
